title: Molecular Fusion Reactor: Organic-Polymer Synthesis
description: Users operate a virtual chemistry workbench where an Avocado represents an organic fatty-acid molecule and Sunglasses represent a polymer chain catalyst. Players must drag reactants into a central reaction vessel, adjust temperature and pressure sliders, trigger bond-breaking and bond-forming steps in the correct sequence, and manage conservation of atomic mass across all transformations. Undo/redo buttons let users reverse missteps in the multi-step synthesis.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) implementing a molecular fusion reactor workbench. Load Avocado.glb as an 'Organic Substrate' (representing oleic acid) and SunglassesKhronos.glb as a 'Polymer Catalyst' (representing a cross-linking agent). Place both on the left side of the scene on labeled pedestals. Center the scene on a translucent glowing cylindrical reaction vessel (glass-like material with emissive rim). Add OrbitControls for camera (initial position z=5, y=3). Use hemisphere light plus two point lights for lab ambiance. Implement these interaction steps: (1) Drag Avocado into the vessel. (2) Drag Sunglasses into the vessel. (3) Adjust a temperature slider (HTML overlay, 200-600K). (4) Adjust a pressure slider (1-10 atm). (5) Click 'Break Bonds' button — vessel glows red, both models separate into 3 floating sub-fragments each (clone meshes, offset positions with tween). (6) Click 'Conserve Mass' — a mass counter displays total atomic mass; user must verify input mass equals fragment mass by clicking a checkbox. (7) Click 'Reform Bonds' — fragments recombine into a new fused product (merge both models together at vessel center, slightly interleaved). (8) Adjust a pH slider (1-14) to stabilize the product. (9) Click 'Extract Product' — product rises out of vessel with particle effects. (10) Click 'Log Result' to record synthesis in an on-screen journal panel. Implement full undo/redo with Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Y that reverts each step, restoring model positions, slider values, and vessel state. Display a running mass-conservation bar showing input vs output mass that must always balance. Use a state machine to enforce step ordering. Add a step-progress indicator at the top. All UI overlays should use semi-transparent dark panels with white text. Animate vessel glow color changes per reaction phase using ShaderMaterial or emissive property tweening.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  avocadoInVessel: boolean,         // whether the Avocado organic substrate is inside the reaction vessel
  sunglassesInVessel: boolean,         // whether the Sunglasses polymer catalyst is inside the reaction vessel
  temperatureKelvin: number,         // current temperature setting in Kelvin (200-600)
  pressureAtm: number,         // current pressure setting in atmospheres (1-10)
  bondsAreBroken: boolean,         // whether molecular bonds have been broken into fragments
  fragmentCount: number,         // total number of visible molecular fragments (0 or 6)
  massConserved: boolean,         // whether user has verified mass conservation checkbox
  bondsReformed: boolean,         // whether fragments have recombined into fused product
  fusedProductVisible: boolean,         // whether the final synthesized product is visible in vessel
  vesselGlowIntensity: number,         // emissive intensity of the reaction vessel (0.0-2.0)
  reactionStepIndex: number,         // current step in the synthesis workflow (0-7)
}
